quick_facts.label: Common university title
quick_facts.value: At the University of Delhi (DU), the programme is titled B.A. (Hons.) Business Economics (BBE).

quick_facts.label: Study pattern
quick_facts.value: DU’s published BBE structure includes core courses through Semesters I–VIII; under the UGC National Credit Framework, a bachelor’s (honours/research) programme is normally four years and eight semesters, while a standard bachelor’s degree is three years and six semesters. The credential and exit structure should be confirmed with the admitting university.

quick_facts.label: DU 2026 admission route
quick_facts.value: For DU undergraduate admission in academic year 2026–27, CUET (UG) 2026 scores are used and applicants must also complete DU’s CSAS(UG)-2026 process; Class XII marks are used for minimum eligibility and, where necessary, tie-breaking.

quick_facts.label: Fees
quick_facts.value: There is no single DU-wide BBE fee: DU directs applicants to the relevant college’s official website because undergraduate fee structures vary by college.

sections.heading: Eligibility and mathematical preparation
paragraphs: Eligibility is institution-specific. For DU’s 2026–27 undergraduate admissions, candidates must take CUET (UG) 2026 and then apply through CSAS(UG)-2026; DU states that programme-specific eligibility controls which CUET subjects count. DU also confirms that an applicant who studied Applied Mathematics in Class XII may apply for B.A. (Hons.) Business Economics, but must select Mathematics as the CUET domain-specific subject for 2026.

The most recently published DU BBE programme-specific combination available in its 2025 undergraduate bulletin was: one CUET language, Mathematics/Applied Mathematics, and the General Aptitude Test. Because subject rules can change by admission cycle, applicants should treat that as dated 2025 context and verify the 2026 Bulletin of Information before registering or making subject choices.
bullets: A Class XII background including Mathematics or Applied Mathematics is particularly important for the DU BBE route.

Passing CMA Foundation is not presented by DU as a stated prerequisite for BBE admission; applicants should rely on the university’s current programme-specific requirements rather than assume that a professional-course pathway substitutes for CUET/CSAS requirements.

sections.heading: Duration and award structure
paragraphs: BBE is commonly encountered at DU as B.A. (Hons.) Business Economics. DU’s current academic-affairs syllabus page publishes BBE discipline-specific core courses across eight semesters, including Semester VII Data Science and Semester VIII Time Series Econometrics. This reflects the four-year undergraduate framework in use for the published DU scheme.

At the national-framework level, UGC’s National Credit Framework describes a three-year/six-semester bachelor’s degree and a four-year/eight-semester bachelor’s degree (honours, research, or engineering). The exact qualification awarded at a particular exit point, fourth-year track, and credit requirements remain university-regulated.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: The published DU BBE core sequence combines economics, quantitative methods, finance, management, and law. Early study includes Microeconomics, Accounting for Managers, Mathematics for Business Economics, Macroeconomics, Financial Institutions and Markets, and Statistics for Business Economics. Later core study includes Corporate Finance, Marketing Management, Basic Econometrics, Organisational Behaviour, Industrial Economics, Operations Research, Business Legislation in India, Environmental Economics, Data Science, and Time Series Econometrics.

This mix supports the degree’s applied orientation: students are trained to use economic reasoning and quantitative tools in business contexts rather than studying economics only as abstract theory. Electives, generic electives, skill courses, assessment methods, and software exposure can differ by university and college.
bullets: Economics: microeconomics, macroeconomics, industrial and environmental economics.
Quantitative analysis: mathematics, statistics, econometrics, operations research, data science, and time-series econometrics.
Business and finance: accounting, financial institutions and markets, corporate finance, marketing, organisational behaviour, and business legislation.

sections.heading: Admission process
paragraphs: For the DU route in 2026–27, the practical sequence is: take CUET (UG) 2026 in the subjects required by the current BBE programme-specific eligibility, then complete CSAS(UG)-2026 for programme and college preferences. DU says CUET scores are considered for admission, while Class XII marks have no general weightage beyond minimum eligibility and tie-breaking.

Applicants should not infer a fixed cutoff, seat count, or closing date from an earlier cycle. DU’s own 2025 bulletin said that seat matrices could vary and directed candidates to college websites for current fee information; programme availability and conditions should therefore be checked in the current official bulletin and college notices.
bullets: Check whether the target college offers B.A. (Hons.) Business Economics in the intended cycle.
Match CUET subject selection exactly to the current programme-specific rule.
Submit the university’s post-CUET allocation application; a CUET score alone is not the complete DU admission process.

sections.heading: Costs and budgeting
paragraphs: Fee planning must be college-specific. As of DU’s 2026 undergraduate FAQ, the university states that undergraduate fee structures vary by college and directs students to each college’s official website. Applicants should budget separately for university/college fees, CUET and application charges where applicable, accommodation, commuting, books, devices, and any optional exam preparation.

Consequently, no single BBE tuition figure is supplied here: an amount quoted without naming a college and admission year would be misleading. Confirm the published fee notice and refund rules of the allocated college before accepting a seat.

sections.heading: Further study, professional credentials, and career directions
paragraphs: BBE can support further study in management, commerce, economics, analytics, and finance, but each destination has its own eligibility and selection process. At DU, BBE/B.A. (Hons.) Business Economics from a recognised university is listed among the qualifying backgrounds for M.Com. programme-specific eligibility; the applicable CUET-PG route and minimum marks remain governed by the current postgraduate bulletin.

For finance-oriented professional development, the CFA Program permits enrolment after a bachelor’s degree, or for undergraduates whose selected Level I window is within 23 months of graduation. The FRM exam has no education or professional prerequisite for registration, although certification requires passing both exam parts and submitting the required work experience under GARP rules.

For public-sector examination planning, the RBI Grade B General-stream 2026 notice accepts a graduation degree of at least three years after Class XII, subject to its stated minimum marks; the notice specifies 60% for General graduation eligibility and 50% for SC/ST/PwBD applicants where posts are reserved. These are recruitment-cycle conditions, not a guarantee of future eligibility or selection.

The curriculum’s economics, statistics, finance, accounting, data-science, and management components are relevant preparation for entry-level analytical and management-track roles such as business analysis, credit analysis, and management trainee roles. Actual hiring criteria, compensation, and job titles vary by employer, location, skills, internships, and recruitment cycle.
bullets: Postgraduate options: M.Com. where programme-specific eligibility is met; management and other postgraduate programmes through their own current entrance and admission rules.
Professional options: CFA Program and FRM, subject to the professional bodies’ current registration, exam, experience, and ethics requirements.
Competitive-exam option: RBI Grade B General stream, subject to the relevant year’s notification and eligibility rules.
Employment preparation: strengthen spreadsheet modelling, statistics/econometrics, data analysis, communication, internships, and evidence of applied projects alongside the degree.
